relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Beloved children imitate the Divine.
Walk in love as Messiah’s self-giving fragrance.
Misused sexuality, uncleanness, and greed do not fit holy ones.
Shameful speech gives way to thanksgiving.
Exploitative desire and greed do not inherit Messiah’s reign.
Empty words must not deceive the body.
Do not co-participate in those patterns.
Once darkness, now light in the Lord.
Light bears fruit as goodness, justice, and truth.
Test what pleases the Lord.
Do not share in unfruitful darkness; expose it.
Secret shame loses its power when light comes.
Light makes things manifest.
Wake, sleeper; rise, and Messiah will shine.
Walk carefully as wise ones.
Redeem the season in harmful days.
Understand the Lord’s will.
Do not be filled with excess; be filled in Spirit.
Spirit-filling becomes shared song and heart-melody.
Thanksgiving rises always to the Divine.
Mutual reverent yielding frames the household.
Wives are addressed within that mutual frame.
Head/source imagery points to Messiah and the body.
The assembly’s relation to Messiah shapes the image.
Husbands are commanded into self-giving Messiah-love.
Love sanctifies and cleanses.
The body is presented radiant, holy, and whole.
To love the wife as one’s own body is to reject domination.
No one hates the body; Messiah nourishes and cherishes the assembly.
We are members of his body.
Two become one flesh.
The marriage image opens into Messiah and the assembly.
Love and reverence close the household image.

contemplative_note: Ephesians 5 moves from light-walk to love-walk. Household language is held inside mutual reverence and Messiah’s self-giving love, never domination.
